emergency_procurement.name: Emergency Procurement
emergency_procurement.label: Emergency / Urgent Procurement
emergency_procurement.description: An expedited process for genuine emergencies or urgent operational needs where normal competitive processes cannot be followed in the available timeframe.
steps: Prepare written emergency justification — document why normal procurement cannot be followed
Obtain emergency approval from the appropriate delegate before committing
Identify and engage the most appropriate supplier directly
Negotiate best available price and document the outcome
Execute contract or purchase order with clear scope, price, and timeframe
Complete a post-emergency review within 30 days — document lessons learned
Ensure all approvals and records are complete for audit purposes

major_strategic.name: Major / Strategic Procurement
major_strategic.label: Major / Strategic Procurement
major_strategic.description: A structured, high-governance process for major procurements that require executive oversight, formal market engagement, and rigorous evaluation.
steps: Develop a detailed Procurement Strategy and obtain approval before going to market
Consider Gateway Review requirements — engage Infrastructure NSW
Undertake market sounding or RFI if the requirement needs further definition
Develop detailed specification, evaluation criteria, and contract terms
Publish approach to market on NSW eTendering
Conduct formal evaluation with a probity advisor
Prepare detailed evaluation report and seek legal review of contract
Obtain executive approvals (Deputy Secretary / Secretary level)
Execute contract and establish governance and performance management arrangements
Publish contract award on NSW eTendering

limited_direct.name: Limited / Direct Procurement
limited_direct.label: Limited Market / Direct Procurement
limited_direct.description: A constrained process used where the market is genuinely limited. Requires documented justification explaining why full competition cannot be achieved.
steps: Prepare direct procurement justification — document limited market evidence
Confirm no existing government arrangement covers this requirement
Obtain approval at one level above standard for this value
Approach 2–3 suppliers if available, or single supplier with documented rationale
Negotiate price and scope — document how value for money is being achieved
Execute contract or purchase order
Record the justification and approval in your procurement file

simple_purchase.name: Simple Purchase
simple_purchase.label: Simple / Low-Value Purchase
simple_purchase.description: A straightforward purchase process for low-value, well-defined requirements. Proportionate and low-administrative burden.
steps: Confirm the requirement is clearly defined
Check whether a government arrangement or standing offer applies
If an arrangement exists: obtain a quote through the arrangement
If no arrangement: identify a suitable supplier and confirm price
Obtain line manager or budget approval as required
Execute purchase order or use procurement card as appropriate
Retain records sufficient for audit (quote, approval, purchase record)

quote_based.name: Quote-Based Procurement
quote_based.label: Competitive Quote Process
quote_based.description: A competitive quote process appropriate for low-to-medium value, well-defined requirements with a broad enough market to generate meaningful competition.
steps: Confirm no existing arrangement covers this requirement
Define your requirement — prepare a brief statement of work or specification
Identify at least 3 suppliers to approach
Issue Request for Quote (RFQ) with clear scope, evaluation criteria, and deadline
Evaluate responses against stated criteria — document your assessment
Select the preferred supplier and document the decision rationale
Obtain required delegate approval
Execute contract or purchase order
Retain evaluation and approval records

structured_market.name: Structured Market Approach
structured_market.label: Structured Market Approach
structured_market.description: A structured process that allows for market engagement and solution development before committing to a specific approach. Suited to complex or partially-defined requirements.
steps: Consider a Request for Information (RFI) or market sounding to refine the requirement
Develop Procurement Strategy with market context — obtain approval
Define evaluation criteria that accommodate solution variation
Publish approach to market on NSW eTendering
Conduct structured evaluation — consider staged or two-envelope process
Engage with preferred supplier(s) to finalise scope and commercial terms
Prepare evaluation report and obtain delegate approval
Execute contract with clear performance and governance arrangements
Publish contract award on NSW eTendering

open_market.name: Open Market Procurement
open_market.label: Open Market Procurement
open_market.description: The default competitive process for medium-to-high value procurements. Open advertising ensures fair access and maximises competition.
steps: Confirm no existing arrangement covers this requirement
Develop Procurement Strategy — document approach, market, and evaluation plan
Obtain Procurement Strategy approval
Develop specification, evaluation criteria, and contract terms
Publish Request for Tender (RFT) on NSW eTendering
Conduct briefing session for potential suppliers if appropriate
Evaluate responses using documented criteria with an evaluation panel
Prepare evaluation report — obtain legal review if required
Obtain delegate approval for contract award
Notify successful and unsuccessful suppliers
Execute contract
Publish contract award on NSW eTendering
